commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Telegram HTML preservation to validate each raw tag through preserveTelegramHtmlTag while tracking supported raw tags for code/pre regions.

PR surface: Source -3. Total -3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: unclear. I did not reproduce the Telegram Web failure, and the PR body has no real Telegram proof. Source inspection shows the changed HTML sanitizer path, but ordinary text sends now go through rich_message, so a real Telegram check is still needed.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof plus confirmation that this is the affected Telegram send path, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve only supported HTML tags in messages to fix Telegram Web client compatibility
target.body: This PR fixes a regression in Telegram message formatting where unsupported HTML tags (especially those with attributes like `<code class="language-python">`) were being blindly included. This caused the Telegram Web client to report messages as unsupported. The fix ensures that every tag is validated using `preserveTelegramHtmlTag` before being added to the output.
target.author: leonmuellerfijucha
